claim_bullets.bullet_text: JavaScript gives us a way to do that, the const keyword (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.51-53).
The const keyword introduces one or more bindings in the block that encloses it (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.51-53).
We use the const keyword in a const statement (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.51-53).
We'll learn a lot more about const in future chapters, but here's the most important thing we can do with const: bind values to names in a block-scoped manner (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.51-53).

source_record_text: The const keyword in JavaScript provides a way to bind values to names in a block-scoped manner, as described in the chapter on const (pages 51-53).
